title: Implement start/resume separation and transaction transition enforcement
summary: Implement the Phase 2 runtime changes for v0.4.13 so transaction start, resume, and lifecycle progression obey one explicit transaction state machine. The work must prevent duplicate non-terminal begin, ensure resume binds to the existing active transaction instead of creating a new one, and enforce canonical transition preconditions before lifecycle, verify, commit, and terminal events are emitted.
outputs: Runtime implementation that separates new transaction start from resume behavior
Explicit enforcement of valid transaction transitions across lifecycle, verify, commit, and terminal operations
Prevention of duplicate non-terminal tx.begin and other specification-violating follow-up flows
Clear integrity failures when a requested action does not match the canonical transaction state machine
acceptance_criteria: A non-terminal transaction cannot receive a second `tx.begin` for the same ticket lineage.
Resume logic binds to the existing active non-terminal transaction and does not emit another `tx.begin`.
Lifecycle progression cannot occur before canonical `tx.begin` has been recorded.
Verify result events cannot be recorded without a canonical prior `tx.verify.start` for the relevant step.
Commit cannot start unless canonical synchronized state confirms a prior successful verify pass.
Terminal transactions reject additional non-terminal follow-up events.
When canonical state is ambiguous or invalid, the implementation fails with an integrity error instead of guessing or self-healing by issuing a new begin.
tasks: Review the current start, resume, lifecycle, verify, commit, and terminal event paths to identify where implicit assumptions bypass the intended state machine.
Implement an explicit distinction between new-start and resume flows so that only true new work emits `tx.begin`.
Add enforcement that prevents duplicate non-terminal `tx.begin` and rejects lifecycle progression before begin.
Ensure verify and commit helpers validate required prior canonical transitions before emitting follow-up events.
Ensure post-terminal follow-up events are rejected consistently across all helpers.
Return direct integrity failures when transition preconditions are not met rather than continuing into secondary downstream errors.
Document any helper-level transition constraints needed to keep start/resume and event progression behavior consistent.
notes: This ticket focuses on Phase 2 transition enforcement, not on weakening validator behavior.
Treat duplicate non-terminal begin as a specification violation, not as a recoverable convenience path.
Resume must reuse canonical active transaction context; it must not synthesize a replacement transaction start because materialized state looks incomplete.
Use the observed failure patterns `tx.begin required before other events`, `commit.start requires verify.pass`, and `duplicate tx.begin` as concrete regression targets for the implementation.
